header: Head of B2B Unit / Quinto Andar

lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- The Opening Act -- Structuring Presentations
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eduardo Chiba | Aula 11 -- Structuring Presentations

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and thank you for being here. I'm Eduardo Chiba, Head of the B2B Unit at Quinto Andar. Let me start by giving you a quick overview of today's presentation. First, I'll cover the market and where the opportunity is. After that, I'll move on to our results from the last quarter. Then I'd like to walk you through our plan for the year ahead. Finally, I'll share the three key takeaways I want you to remember. The presentation will take about twenty minutes, and there will be time for questions at the end. So, to begin, let's look at the market. The B2B real estate segment is growing fast, and the audience for our product is larger than ever. Keep that picture in mind, because everything I show you today connects back to it.

Okay, this is just a quick rehearsal of my opening. Let me try it again. Good afternoon, everyone, and thanks for joining. I'd like to begin by giving you a short overview of where we are. First, I'll set the scene with the market. After that, I'll move on to the numbers. Then I'll walk you through the plan, and finally I'll leave you with three clear takeaways. I think the structure is good, but I am speaking too fast. I need to slow down and pause after each signpost, so the audience can follow the transition. The opening sets the tone for the whole presentation, so it has to be calm and confident. One more time, slower this time, and then I'll be ready.

extra_audio.text: First, you open and give a short overview of the whole presentation. Then you set the scene and explain why the topic matters to the audience. After that, you walk the audience through each main point in order. Next, you signpost every transition, so people always know where they are. Finally, you sum up the key takeaways and invite questions.
